hypothesis.claim: Reframing the primary_text to speak to the reader's felt need (not product features) should raise motivation_match from 2/5.

variant.primary_text:
Ever wonder what your kids are actually eating at dinner?

We checked the supply chain on supermarket produce labelled 'fresh.' Most of it sat in cold storage for weeks before it hit the shelf. Two NSW farms passed the test we wish every parent could see.

They're the ones quietly feeding families on the Northern Beaches who got tired of guessing. Order Sunday, collect Wednesday from Brookvale. Know the farm. Know the week it was picked. Stop wondering.
variant.headline: Why do 9 of 10 'fresh' veggies fail this test?
variant.description: Groceries from named NSW farms. Collect Brookvale.

hypothesis.claim: Removing competing themes and focusing every sentence on one proposition should raise angle_clarity from 3/5.

variant.primary_text:
How many of your neighbours could name the farm that grew their dinner?

Almost none. We barely could either, until we started picking up groceries from the same handful of NSW farms every week.

Now we know who grows the carrots. Who packs the eggs. The kids ask for them by name.

Quietly, more Northern Beaches families are doing the same thing every Saturday. Order online, collect at Brookvale, meet the people behind the food.
variant.headline: How many neighbours know their farmer?
variant.description: Named NSW farms. Saturday pickup, Brookvale.

hypothesis.claim: Replacing the cta with an outcome-stated version should raise cta_clarity from 2/5.

variant.primary_text:
Do you know the names of the farmers who grew this week's dinner?

Most Sydney families can't name one. The families collecting from Brookvale on Saturday can name three — and they'll tell you why it matters the moment you taste the difference.

We're not a supermarket. We're a hub: NSW farms drop off Friday, you collect Saturday, your kids meet the people who grow their food.

Order by Thursday, collect Saturday from Brookvale — and we'll text you the farmer's name with every order so you know exactly who fed your family this week.
variant.headline: Which 3 NSW farms feed your family?
variant.description: Order online by Thursday, collect Saturday from Brookvale. Farmer names included.

hypothesis.claim: Adding soft-scarcity signals (first access, registration opens soon, limited spots) should raise scarcity_register from 3/5.

variant.primary_text:
Most of us don't. We pick up an apple, a punnet of strawberries, a bag of carrots, and we couldn't name the farm or the family if we tried.

Then there are the families who can.

They know Tom grows their sweet potatoes in Wauchope. They know the Higgins family picks their peaches in Bellingen. They check in on the spring harvest the way you'd check in on a friend.

It's a quieter way to feed a family, and once you've shopped this way it's hard to go back.

Brookvale collection opens again soon. Add your name and we'll tell you the moment a slot is yours.
variant.headline: Do you know who grew your dinner?
variant.description: Farm-direct groceries from NSW growers you can name. Order online, collect from Brookvale.
